content: The state department has also authorised non-emergency embassy staff to leave on security grounds.

content: The foreign minister says the accusations, involving children, were investigated and found to be unsubstantiated.

originalTitle: Caf president would welcome corruption investigation
title: Caf president would welcome corruption investigation
content: Confederation of African Football president Patrice Motsepe has "zero tolerance" for corruption, which he says is "worse than cancer".

title: Israeli attacks on Lebanon aimed to undermine ceasefire, critics say
content: More than 250 people have been killed in a barrage of air strikes on Lebanon since the ceasefire was declared.

content: The proposal fuels uncertainty as US officials offer conflicting interpretations of the plan to permanently end hostilities.

content: Brazil's Laura Cardoso takes record nine-wicket haul in 189 win against Lesotho to claim best bowling figures in a T20I.

content: The US president says Nato "wasn't there when we needed them" during the Iran war.

title: At least 182 killed across Lebanon in large wave of Israeli strikes
content: Attacks hit the southern suburbs of Beirut, southern Lebanon and the eastern Bekaa Valley, hours after a US-Iran ceasefire was announced.

title: Scotland confirm final World Cup warm-up against Bolivia - live on BBC
content: Scotland confirm a friendly against Bolivia in Harrison, New Jersey on 6 June as their final warm-up game before this summer's World Cup finals.
